_note: Brand-authorable content for the public, host-global 'Features' CMS page (ADR 0027). Seeded by host/featuresPage.ts → published at /p/features. Shape: hero, about (richText), howItWorks (numbered steps), groups[] (each a card grid: eyebrow + heading + lede + features[{name, tagline, appPath, icon, optional?}]), stats[], cta. `icon` is a slug from the ui/icons set, mapped in SectionRenderer's ICON_BY_SLUG. Written for a first-time visitor who knows nothing about the app: orient first (what is it / how it works / start here), then the full catalog. Bump featuresPage.ts SEED_VERSION after editing so a redeploy refreshes the live page (only while it has never been hand-edited in the CMS).
title: Features

hero.heading: One place to put AI to work.
hero.subheading: Build AI agents and automated workflows that handle real tasks — then run, watch, and stay in control of all of it. New here? Start with the three essentials, and switch on the rest when you need them.

about.heading: What is OpenWOP?
about.body:
OpenWOP is a place to put AI to work on real tasks — answering questions, drafting documents, updating records, and running multi-step jobs on a schedule.

You build two things: **agents** (AI coworkers, each with a job to do) and **workflows** (the steps they follow). Then you run them, watch every step, and decide what needs your sign-off. It's a working app *and* an open standard, so what you build stays yours — portable, not locked to one vendor. Everything below is built in; features marked **optional** are add-ons you can switch on when you need them.

howItWorks.heading: From a plain-language ask to work done.

steps.title: Say what you need
steps.text: Ask an agent in plain language, the way you'd message a colleague.

steps.title: It does the work
steps.text: The agent runs a workflow — steps you design on a visual canvas, or use ready-made.

steps.title: Watch it happen
steps.text: Follow every step live, and replay any run to see exactly what it did.

steps.title: Stay in control
steps.text: Approve anything sensitive before it runs. Your data and AI keys stay yours.

groups.heading: The three essentials
groups.lede: New here? These three are the heart of it — everything else builds on them.

features.name: Chat
features.tagline: Talk to your agents in plain language and start work from one place.

features.name: Agents
features.tagline: Named AI coworkers — each with its own job, schedule, and to-do board.

features.name: Workflows
features.tagline: Design multi-step automations on a drag-and-drop canvas.

groups.heading: Keep work moving
groups.lede: Containers, boards, and channels to organize what your agents and your team are doing.

features.name: Projects
features.tagline: Group work into containers with their own board, memory, and workflows.

features.name: Boards
features.tagline: Plan work on a kanban board where a card can trigger a run.

features.name: Inbox
features.tagline: One place for everything that needs you — approvals, blockers, and alerts.

features.name: Channels
features.tagline: Team channels for real-time messaging alongside your agents.

features.name: Scheduled chats
features.tagline: Schedule recurring agent check-ins — digests, monitors, standups.

groups.heading: See and control the work
groups.lede: Watch every run as it happens, set the guardrails, and keep a human in the loop.

features.name: Mission Control
features.tagline: Watch your whole fleet of runs live, in one view.

features.name: Runs
features.tagline: Review every run's history and detail — and replay it.

features.name: Workforces
features.tagline: Govern clusters of agents with a shared purpose, limits, and telemetry.

features.name: Agent templates
features.tagline: Install ready-made agents and packs.

features.name: Org chart
features.tagline: Map your roster and reporting lines.

features.name: Capabilities
features.tagline: See exactly what this host supports.

features.name: Memory
features.tagline: Inspect what your agents remember, attributed per tenant.

features.name: Model leaderboard
features.tagline: Rank model quality from real feedback, head-to-head.

features.name: Usage
features.tagline: Track token usage and cost per model.

groups.heading: Give agents what they need to know
groups.lede: Feed agents trusted sources, and turn their work into documents, pages, and plans.

features.name: Knowledge Base
features.tagline: Upload documents and get answers with cited sources you can check.

features.name: Notebooks
features.tagline: Research notebooks — gather sources, take notes, and ask grounded questions.

features.name: Podcasts
features.tagline: Turn a notebook into a multi-speaker audio overview you can listen to.

features.name: Documents
features.tagline: Draft business documents from templates — SOWs, PRDs, RFPs, agendas.

features.name: Prompts
features.tagline: Save reusable prompt templates with variables.

features.name: Media
features.tagline: Keep your images and files in one shared asset library.

features.name: CMS
features.tagline: Build pages with a typed page builder — this page was made with it.

features.name: Publishing
features.tagline: Put pages on a public site with search-engine basics handled.

features.name: Priority Matrix
features.tagline: Score and rank ideas, then plan what to do next.

features.name: Strategy
features.tagline: Set objectives and OKRs that tie your projects, priorities, and advisors together.

features.name: Board of Advisors
features.tagline: Convene councils of advisor agents for a second opinion.

groups.heading: Reach and serve customers
groups.lede: Optional add-ons to run sales, support, and marketing where your agents already work.

features.name: CRM
features.tagline: Track contacts, companies, and deals in one pipeline.

features.name: Customer Success
features.tagline: Keep customer-success accounts healthy and on track.

features.name: Forms
features.tagline: Publish forms that drop new leads straight into your CRM.

features.name: Email
features.tagline: Run templated email campaigns to your contacts.

features.name: Analytics
features.tagline: See traffic and conversions on your public pages.

features.name: Consent
features.tagline: Handle region-aware consent and data-subject requests (GDPR).

features.name: Comments
features.tagline: Add threaded comments to pages and collections.

groups.heading: Run it your way
groups.lede: Connect your tools, bring your own AI keys, and manage who can do what.

features.name: Connections
features.tagline: Securely link Google, Slack, Zoom, and more.

features.name: Keys
features.tagline: Bring your own provider keys — encrypted and under your control.

features.name: Organizations
features.tagline: Manage orgs, teams, members, and roles.

features.name: Users
features.tagline: Manage accounts and identity.

features.name: Feature toggles
features.tagline: Turn features on, off, or beta — and split traffic.

features.name: CLI
features.tagline: Drive the platform from your terminal.

features.name: Marketplace
features.tagline: Browse and install signed feature packs.

features.name: Sharing
features.tagline: Share pages and collections with public links.

cta.heading: See it for yourself.
cta.subheading: Explore the live app — no setup required — or read the open standard behind it.
